entries.citation: kapustin-fidkowski — Kapustin & Fidkowski, "Local Commuting Projector Hamiltonians and the Quantum Hall Effect", Commun. Math. Phys. 373, 763 (2019/2020), arXiv:1810.07756
entries.notes: Web-verified in this project's prior Part V citation audit (bordism-realizability-citation.json). Cross-checked directly this session against Part V's own formal Theorem V-C (bordism-realizability.tex, lines 777-799), which states the no-go as an explicit TWO-PART result: (i) electric, U(1)-symmetric, sigma_H=0 [Kapustin-Fidkowski]; (ii) thermal, no symmetry hypothesis, c_-=0 [Kapustin-Spodyneiko, kapustin-spodyneiko-thermal], with Part V stating in its own words that 'the two obstructions are logically independent' and that 'the bosonic E_8 state has sigma_H=0 (no U(1) charge) yet c_-=8.'
entries.explanation: SPOT-VERIFIED load-bearing attribution, FLAGGED ISSUE. Synthesis's Recollection (Sec. 3.5, label rec:VC, lines 737-743) is titled 'Kapustin-Fidkowski no-go' and cites only {kapustin-fidkowski, paperRealizability} -- it never cites kapustin-spodyneiko-thermal anywhere in the paper (confirmed: the key does not appear once in synthesis.tex's full \cite list, despite being used 4 times in the Part V source it is restating). Yet the recollection's own text asserts both halves of the joint theorem ('zero ground-state Hall conductance AND zero chiral central charge') and then states 'every invertible phase with sigma_H != 0 ... the bosonic E_8 state ... is not commuting-projector realizable.' This is factually backwards relative to Part V's own explicit statement: the E_8 state has sigma_H=0 (it carries no U(1) charge) and is excluded solely by the chiral-central-charge clause (ii), which is Kapustin-Spodyneiko's result, not Kapustin-Fidkowski's -- a distinction Part V goes out of its way to state ('The two obstructions are logically independent'). The identical conflation recurs in the Introduction/stance section (Sec. 1.3, lines 362-365: 'Kapustin-Fidkowski ... proved that no local commuting-projector Hamiltonian carries a nonzero Hall conductance, so every chiral invertible class lies outside the commuting-projector image'), which attributes the whole two-author, two-mechanism 'no-go wall' to Kapustin-Fidkowski alone. Net effect: a real, correctly-existing citation is used to over-broadly cover content that is actually a distinct, separately-authored and separately-cited theorem in the very module paper being restated, and the paper's own worked example (E_8) is misclassified under the wrong mechanism as a result. This is drift introduced during synthesis compression, not a fabricated source.

entries.explanation: FLAGGED ISSUE, part of the Ogata spot-check (see ogata-h3-index entry for full detail). Used three times: (1) Recollection III (Sec. 3.3, Conjecture III-3, correctly hedged as identifying Weq-components with 'Ogata's operator-algebraic phases' -- fine); (2) homotopy dictionary (Sec. 5, same hedged use -- fine); (3) Recollection V-B (Sec. 3.5, rec:VB) co-cited with ogata-h3-index and ogata-spt-chains for a claim that overstates what Part V itself proves. See ogata-h3-index for the substantive finding.

entries.citation: ogata-h3-index — Ogata, "A H^3(G,T)-valued index of symmetry protected topological phases with on-site finite group symmetry for two-dimensional quantum spin systems", Forum Math. Pi 9, e13 (2021), arXiv:2101.00426
entries.notes: Web-verified in this project's prior Part V citation audit (bordism-realizability-citation.json), which confirmed the abstract establishes existence of an H^3(G,T)-valued index but does not itself assert completeness.
entries.explanation: SPOT-VERIFIED load-bearing attribution, FLAGGED ISSUE. Synthesis's Recollection (Sec. 3.5, label rec:VB, lines 728-735, 'Low-dimensional completeness') states: 'the H^2(G,U(1))-valued index in d=1 and the H^3(G,U(1))-valued index of Ogata in d=2 are complete invariants of the symmetric phase [cites ogata-h3-index, ogata-spt-chains, ogata-classification-review]; there real is a bijection onto the group-cohomology classification, so realized = invariant.' This directly contradicts Part V's own Theorem V-B and its two dedicated companion remarks (bordism-realizability.tex: Theorem V-B at thm:VB states d=1 completeness only; Remark 'Two dimensions: the index exists, completeness is open' at rem:VB2d states verbatim 'Ogata's theorem gives existence and invariance of the index, not completeness. We therefore record d=2 completeness as an open problem, not a theorem, and do not assert realized = invariant in d=2'; and a further Remark 'Scope -- do not extrapolate' at rem:VBscope). Synthesis's rec:VB erases exactly the distinction these two remarks were written to enforce, upgrading an explicitly open d=2 problem to an established bijection/completeness result. This is corroborated by an internal inconsistency within synthesis.tex itself: three other passages that reference \Cref{rec:VB} (Sec. 4.1's composition-diagram discussion, and Conjecture VI-2's body in Sec. 8) both correctly restrict the claim to 'in d=1 for on-site finite symmetry,' showing the paper's own later usage understands the correct scope even though the boxed Recollection VB itself does not state it. This is drift introduced during synthesis compression of Part V's Theorem V-B / Remark V-B', not a fabricated or nonexistent source -- the citations themselves are genuine and their d=1 use is correct; only the d=2 half of the claim is unsupported by the very sources cited for it.

missing_references: kapustin-spodyneiko-thermal (Kapustin & Spodyneiko, "Thermal Hall conductance and a relative topological invariant of gapped two-dimensional systems", Phys. Rev. B 101, 235130 (2020), arXiv:1905.06488) is never cited anywhere in synthesis.tex, despite already being present and verified in the shared references.bib and cited 4 times in the Part V companion paper (bordism-realizability.tex) as the source of the 'zero chiral central charge' half of Theorem V-C -- the specific mechanism that excludes the bosonic E_8 state (which the source paper explicitly notes has sigma_H=0). Synthesis's Recollection VC (Sec. 3.5, lines 737-743) and its Introduction/stance section (Sec. 1.3, lines 362-365) both assert this exact chiral-central-charge content and both name the E_8 state as an example, under the citation and title 'Kapustin-Fidkowski no-go' alone. Recommend adding \cite{kapustin-spodyneiko-thermal} to both passages and either retitling Recollection VC (e.g. 'Kapustin-Fidkowski/Kapustin-Spodyneiko no-go') or explicitly splitting the two-part statement as Part V itself does, so the E_8 exclusion is attributed to the correct (thermal/chiral) mechanism rather than implied to follow from nonzero electric Hall conductance.
summary: All 36 unique \cite keys used in synthesis.tex resolve to entries in the shared references.bib (verified programmatically), and the compiled PDF (papers/pdf/synthesis.pdf) contains zero unresolved '[?]' citation markers. No fabricated or novel sources were found: all 30 non-companion keys were cross-checked and each already appears in at least one of the five prior, independently citation-audited module papers, confirming the author's claim that no new sources were added for this capstone. All 6 companion-paper citation keys (paperLocality, paperPositivity, paperGap, paperEFT, paperRealizability, and the correctly-absent paperSynthesis) were checked at every one of their combined 23 appearances in the text: each is paired with its correct Part number with no cross-wiring, and paperSynthesis is never cited, confirming the paper does not cite itself. Of the five requested load-bearing restatement spot-checks, three are clean: Aoki's solidification theorem (Recollection, Sec. 3.2) and Kubota's Omega-spectrum theorem (Recollection, Sec. 3.4) are both faithful, near-verbatim restatements of Part II's and Part IV's own theorem statements respectively, including Aoki's Bott-inversion caveat. The other two show genuine, well-evidenced drift introduced during compression, both concentrated in the paper's treatment of Part V. First, Recollection V-B (Sec. 3.5, 'Low-dimensional completeness') states that Ogata's d=2 H^3(G,U(1)) index is a complete invariant with the realizability map a bijection, directly contradicting Part V's own Theorem V-B and two dedicated remarks ('the index exists, completeness is open' and 'Scope -- do not extrapolate'), which explicitly forbid asserting realized=invariant in d=2; this is corroborated by three other passages within synthesis.tex itself that correctly restrict the same Recollection's scope to d=1 only. Second, Recollection V-C ('Kapustin-Fidkowski no-go', Sec. 3.5) merges two logically-independent, separately-authored mechanisms -- Kapustin-Fidkowski's U(1)-symmetric electric-Hall no-go and Kapustin-Spodyneiko's symmetry-free chiral-central-charge no-go -- under a single citation and title, omitting \cite{kapustin-spodyneiko-thermal} even while asserting its content, and consequently misclassifies the bosonic E_8 state (which Part V explicitly states has zero electric Hall conductance) as an example of nonzero-Hall-conductance exclusion rather than the chiral-central-charge exclusion Part V actually invokes for it; the identical conflation recurs in the Introduction. Both issues are attribution/compression drift relative to an already-clean module source, not fabrication, and both are isolated to the paper's restatement of Part V (Recollections VB and VC); all other recollections, including the two additional Part V theorems checked incidentally (V-A and the stability trio of Part III), were found faithful.
